name: bshlib

metadata.name: bshlib
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)

documentation:
# summary

bashiron personal miscellaneous scripts.

functions.name: bshlib.utils.pathlike_compatible
documentation: Convert `PathLike` function args to `str`.
----- DECORATORS
TODO: other possible behavior: the decorator takes a string param with the name of the PathLike param to convert
TODO: could also take a param that defines whether to convert to `Path` or to `str`

functions.name: bshlib.utils.get_methods
documentation: Expose object methods.
----- FUNCTIONS

functions.name: bshlib.utils.gsub_file
documentation: Apply substitution to an entire file.
TODO: eventually use the 'regex' pypi package for extended regex operations

functions.name: bshlib.utils.vid_duration
documentation:
Return video duration in seconds.

Parameters
-----
video : `os.PathLike[str]` or `str`
    Path to video.

Returns
-----
ret : `int`
    Number of seconds.

functions.name: bshlib.utils.real_vid_birthtime
documentation:
In the case of videos which birthtime is not available will calculate real creation/birth time by subtracting the
duration of the video from the modification time.

Parameters
-----
video : `os.PathLike[str]` or `str`
    Path to video.
tz : `timezone`
    Timezone to convert birthtime timestamp to.

Returns
-----
ret : `datetime`
    Real creation/birth date of video.

functions.name: bshlib.utils.hour_capped_duration
documentation:
Return a named tuple created from an amount of seconds, capped at hours.

Parameters
-----
seconds : int
    Amount of seconds to convert.

Returns
-----
ret : Hdur
    Hdur object containing hours, minutes and seconds.

functions.name: bshlib.utils.super_copyfile
documentation:
Copy file to destination, creating all intermediate directories if needed.

Parameters
-----
src : `os.PathLike[str]` or `str`
    Path to file.
dst : `os.PathLike[str]` or `str`
    Destination directory.

functions.name: bshlib.utils.super_touch
documentation:
Create file and all parent directories.

Parameters
-----
_file : `Path`
    File.

functions.name: bshlib.utils.req_path
documentation:
Check for Path existance.

Parameters
-----
path : `Path`
    Path.
----- VALIDATION

classes.name: bshlib.utils.Hdur
documentation: Time duration structure capped at hours, containing hours, minutes and seconds.

classes.name: bshlib.utils._GetchUnix
documentation: For getting 1 character from stdin.
----- CLASSES

functions.name: bshlib.output_mgr.task_op
documentation:
Check that `task` attribute is set. Can only be used to decorate `OutputMgr`'s methods since it takes an
`OutputMgr` object as first argument.

methods.name: bshlib.output_mgr.OutputMgr.__init__
documentation:
Parameters
-----
out_root : `PathLike[str]` or `str`
    Output root from where the manager will operate.

methods.name: bshlib.output_mgr.OutputMgr.switch
documentation: Switch to operating on `task`.

methods.name: bshlib.output_mgr.OutputMgr.mkfile
documentation:
Opens file in its appropiate task folder. The file is appended with a number.

Returns
-----
ret : `TextIO`
    Open file handle.

methods.name: bshlib.output_mgr.OutputMgr.create
documentation:
Creates file in its appropiate task folder. The file is appended with a number. Fills the file with
provided content.

Parameters
-----
content : `Any`
    Content to write to file.

Returns
-----
ret : `Path`
    Path of the created file.

methods.name: bshlib.output_mgr.OutputMgr.clear
documentation: Clears task directory.

methods.name: bshlib.output_mgr.OutputMgr.conventional
documentation:
Return a list of paths of every file that follows the numeric sequence filename convention.

Returns
-----
ret : `list` of `Path`
    Paths of conventional files.

methods.name: bshlib.output_mgr.OutputMgr.rearrange
documentation:
Rename the task files so they follow a sequential order again. Useful for gaps in the numeric naming sequence
caused by file deletion.

methods.name: bshlib.output_mgr.OutputMgr.next_num
documentation: Get next file number for task file.

methods.name: bshlib.output_mgr.OutputMgr.extract_number
documentation:
Extract the sequence number from the file.

Parameters
-----
path : `Path`
    Path of the file to extract from.

methods.name: bshlib.output_mgr.OutputMgr.__strong_open
documentation:
Open file, creating parent directory if doesn't exist.

Returns
-----
ret : `TextIO`
    Open file handle.

modules.name: bshlib._version
documentation: file generated by setuptools_scm
don't change, don't track in version control

modules.name: bshlib.shell_curator
documentation: TODO: lookup how a library like [jc](https://github.com/kellyjonbrazil/jc) or nushell curates shell output

functions.name: bshlib.shell_curator.curate_output
documentation:
Curate text output of shell command. *Don't* use for binary output.

Parameters
-----
string : `str`
    Text ouput.

Returns
-----
list[str]
    List of lines of output.
